description_long: This town hall complex features a series of interconnected buildings with sloped, reddish-brown tiled roofs and white or light-colored facades. The main structure is surrounded by a large open plaza and tree-lined walkways, with several smaller annexes and courtyards visible within the perimeter. The facades are punctuated by regularly spaced windows and subtle decorative elements, giving the building a traditional yet civic appearance. The grounds include landscaped areas with rows of trees, benches, and paved paths, suggesting a welcoming public space. The overall layout is rectangular, with the main entrance facing a broad open area and secondary access points along the sides. The architectural style is reminiscent of Mediterranean or Southern European civic buildings, with a focus on symmetry and community gathering spaces.
description: A large, traditional town hall complex with tiled roofs, white facades, and tree-lined plazas, featuring multiple interconnected buildings and landscaped public areas.
description_view_0: This view highlights the main entrance, the broad open plaza, and the prominent row of trees leading up to the building.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the length of the complex, the continuous row of trees, and the series of windows and annexes along the facade.
description_view_2: This angle shows the rear courtyard, additional green spaces, and the secondary buildings within the complex.
description_view_3: This perspective displays the full frontage, the main entrance, and the landscaped area with benches and trees in front of the building.
attribute_architectural_style: Mediterranean Revival with traditional civic influences
attribute_facade_ornamentation: Minimal ornamentation with regular window spacing, subtle cornices, and tiled roof edges
attribute_entrance_design: Main entrance features double doors, a small portico, and steps leading up from the plaza, framed by simple columns and decorative trim
